source_jobs.title: Senior SWE, Full-Stack
source_jobs.description:
Highlight is building a shared intelligence layer for the modern workforce. Highlight unifies context across every person and tool on your team, seamlessly bridging information silos. It evolves with your organization to proactively route knowledge and reliably automate workflows.



The Role

We're seeking a Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer to take ownership of native app aspects across the board. Highlight’s core product depends on capturing context from the user’s device, and we’re looking for a software engineer to own runtime and make it production-grade: low-latency, lightweight, and highly reliable.

This is a highly technical role for someone who’s excited to work at the intersection of desktop software, native development, and AI integration, and who thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.

Note: this is an on-site role, requiring five days a week in our office.



Job Responsibilities

 - Own the architecture and implementation of Highlight’s native desktop runtime across macOS and Windows

 - Drive improvements to performance, stability, and resource efficiency across native processes

 - Implement robust telemetry, diagnostics, and monitoring to detect regressions and accelerate debugging

 - Collaborate with backend engineering, ML engineering, and product to deliver end-to-end features across the desktop app and backend systems

 - Establish best practices for native development, testing, release processes, and cross-platform compatibility



Profile

We’re looking for someone who is interested in building the AI user experience for the future. We punch above our weight so we value extreme ownership, accountability, and proactiveness.

This role will focus on hard systems problems—ASR reliability, native event capture performance, OCR/accessibility latency, and on-device inference—so Highlight can scale to more users without regressions in responsiveness or quality. The right engineer will be comfortable debugging OS-specific edge cases, improving telemetry and observability, and shipping features that require deep integration with native APIs.

You are a good fit if you have:

 - 5+ years of software engineering experience, with significant experience shipping native desktop software.

 - Strong systems fundamentals: you write clean, maintainable code and care deeply about performance and reliability.

 - Experience building and debugging macOS and/or Windows native systems.

 - Track record of improving latency, CPU/memory usage, and stability in production applications.

 - Comfort owning systems end-to-end: architecture, implementation, instrumentation, and shipping safely across platforms.

 - A bias toward action: you ship quickly, measure impact, and iterate based on real user feedback.

 - Data driven orientation: Be able to effectively measure what you’ve built.

 - Ownership mentality (no task is too small for you) – it’s all hands on deck here and we need everyone rowing the boat.

 - Excellent communication skills and a collaborative mindset – we’re extremely lean so we’re looking for a team player who works well with others.

 - Type-A tendencies: maybe you’re extremely detail-oriented. Or maybe you take pride in shipping high-quality products. Whatever it is you do, you give it your all.

 - Must be based in or willing to relocate to SF – although we’re flexible with days off & schedules, we have a 100% in-office culture during the week.

And even if you don’t fall neatly into any of these buckets, we’re looking for scrappy and motivated self-starters above all. Hustle is expected, grit is required.



Perks

 - Competitive salary and generous equity package

 - Health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Flexible PTO and parental leave

 - Paid team lunches and dinners during the week

 - Relocation package

source_jobs.title: Head of AI
source_jobs.description:
Highlight is building a shared intelligence layer for the modern workforce. Highlight unifies context across every person and tool on your team, seamlessly bridging information silos. It evolves with your organization to proactively route knowledge and reliably automate workflows.



The Role

We're hiring a Head of AI Engineering to lead and scale our ML and AI efforts as we build the engine that powers Highlight. You'll own the architecture, quality, and roadmap for how we ingest, model, and surface context, starting from data pipelines and evals to the context graph that makes our system truly useful. This is a hands-on leadership role: you'll write code, set technical direction, mentor engineers, and collaborate closely with Backend and Product teams to ship features that feel like magic.

We're looking for a technical leader who's equally comfortable diving into model architectures and rallying a team around ambitious goals. You have deep expertise in LLMs, knowledge systems, and retrieval. You're energized by the challenge of building something new. You thrive in ambiguity, ship fast, and hold yourself and your team to a high bar.

Note: this is an on-site role, requiring five days a week in our office.



Job Responsibilities

 - Own the context graph: architect and build Highlight's memory systems end-to-end, including data ingestion, modeling, retrieval, and evals

 - Research, prototype and deploy state-of-the-art machine learning text and OCR models (e.g., transformer architectures, computer vision, time series forecasting, reinforcement learning)

 - Lead the full ML project lifecycle: data pipelines, training, evals, deployment, monitoring, scalability, latency, retraining, A/B testing, and model lifecycle management

 - Own output quality across Highlight chat, actions, and workflows; drive toward industry leading acceptance rates.

 - Build and lead a world-class ML Engineering team; Establish AI/ML best practices across the broader engineering org.

 - Stay current on knowledge representation, continual learning, and retrieval; translate insights into features that feel like magic; Research novel approaches and publish when appropriate.



Profile

We’re looking for someone who is interested in building the AI user experience for the future. We punch above our weight so we value extreme ownership, accountability, and proactiveness. The ideal candidate has deep, experienced understanding of LLMs or Knowledge systems, and a track record of shipping production AI systems at scale.

You are a good fit if you have:

 - 7+ years in ML/AI engineering, with 3+ years leading teams or major technical initiatives

 - Master’s or PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, Applied Math or equivalent experience.

 - Published research (or experience shipping to production) in knowledge graphs, memory systems, retrieval, or continual learning

 - Experience working at top AI labs (e.g. DeepMind, OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta FAIR, xAI) or leading AI application companies (e.g. Google, Cursor, Perplexity)

 - Strong architectural chops: you've designed and scaled ML infrastructure from scratch

 - Hands-on proficiency with modern ML stacks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, RAG, vector DBs, cloud infra)

 - A player-coach mentality: you love mentoring engineers and getting your hands dirty in the code

 - Ownership mentality (no task is too small for you) – it’s all hands on deck here and we need everyone rowing the boat.

 - Excellent communication skills and a collaborative mindset – we’re extremely lean so we’re looking for a team player who works well with others.

 - Type-A tendencies: maybe you’re extremely detail-oriented. Or maybe you take pride in shipping high-quality products. Whatever it is you do, you give it your all.

 - Must be based in or willing to relocate to SF – although we’re flexible with days off & schedules, we have a 100% in-office culture during the week.

And even if you don’t fall neatly into any of these

source_jobs.title: Senior SWE, Backend
source_jobs.description:
Highlight is building a shared intelligence layer for the modern workforce. Highlight unifies context across every person and tool on your team, seamlessly bridging information silos. It evolves with your organization to proactively route knowledge and reliably automate workflows.



The Role

We're hiring a Senior Backend Software Engineer to own critical backend infrastructure: the systems behind team management, permissions, LLM efficiency, and integration-driven workflows. You’ll scale our systems with a strong emphasis on performance, reliability, and seamless user experience.

This is a highly technical role for someone who’s excited to work at the intersection of desktop software, native development, and AI integration, and who thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.

Note: this is an on-site role, requiring five days a week in our office.



Job Responsibilities

 - Build backend systems that support product growth, including multi-tenant foundations, permissions, and internal/admin tooling

 - Design and operate asynchronous processing systems for background workloads and workflow orchestration

 - Improve system performance and cost through practical optimizations (caching, batching, efficient data access patterns)

 - Build secure, extensible integration infrastructure (authentication, APIs, third-party connectors)

 - Raise the bar on backend engineering quality through observability, testing, incident readiness, and operational best practices

 - Collaborate cross-functionally to ship features end-to-end while designing backend systems that are durable, extensible, and easy to iterate on



Profile

We’re looking for someone who is interested in building the AI user experience for the future. We punch above our weight so we value extreme ownership, accountability, and proactiveness.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building scalable systems, improving observability, and reducing cost through practical optimizations like caching, batching, rollups, and queue-based processing. They’ll partner closely with product and the ML team to ensure Highlight remains reliable and performant as usage scales.



You are a good fit if you have:

 - 5+ years of experience at a fast-moving startup with experience shipping production backend systems

 - Comfort owning systems end-to-end: from architecture and implementation to deployment, monitoring, and debugging

 - Experience building reliable distributed systems (APIs, databases, async jobs, queues, pipelines)

 - Experience building real-time systems (WebSockets, pub/sub, event fanout) with low-latency and reliability at scale

 - Experience building enterprise-ready multi-tenant systems, including team management, roles/permissions, and admin/internal tooling

 - Familiarity with LLM product infrastructure patterns (caching, batching, evals, integrations) is a plus

 - Data driven orientation: Be able to effectively measure what you’ve built.

 - Ownership mentality (no task is too small for you) – it’s all hands on deck here and we need everyone rowing the boat.

 - Excellent communication skills and a collaborative mindset – we’re extremely lean so we’re looking for a team player who works well with others.

 - Type-A tendencies: maybe you’re extremely detail-oriented. Or maybe you take pride in shipping high-quality products. Whatever it is you do, you give it your all.

 - Must be based in or willing to relocate to SF – although we’re flexible with days off & schedules, we have a 100% in-office culture during the week.

And even if you don’t fall neatly into any of these buckets, we’re looking for scrappy and motivated self-starters above all. Hustle is expected, grit is required.



Perks

 - Competitive salary and generous equity package

 - Health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Flexible PTO and parental leave

 - Paid team lunches and dinners during the week

 - Relocation package

source_jobs.title: Senior ML Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Highlight is building a shared intelligence layer for the modern workforce. Highlight unifies context across every person and tool on your team, seamlessly bridging information silos. It evolves with your organization to proactively route knowledge and reliably automate workflows.



The Role

We're hiring a Senior ML Engineer to help build the AI systems that power Highlight. You will work across the full ML stack: data pipelines, model training, retrieval, ranking, evals, and deployment. You will ship features that make Highlight feel like magic. This is a hands on IC role where you will own critical systems end to end and collaborate closely with our Head of AI Engineering to scale our ML capabilities.

You will thrive here if you love shipping production ML systems, obsess over output quality, and want to work on hard problems with real user impact. We move fast, hold a high bar, and believe the best ideas win regardless of title.

Note: this is an on site role, requiring five days a week in our office.



Job Responsibilities

 - Design, build, and optimize ML pipelines for context retrieval, action detection, and output generation

 - Implement and iterate on RAG systems, ranking models, and prompt engineering to improve quality

 - Build comprehensive evals and monitoring to measure and improve ML system performance

 - Investigate alternative models and fine tuning approaches for our heavy background processing workloads, implementing better systems to increase quality while improving cost efficiency

 - Collaborate with Backend and Product Engineering to ship AI powered features

 - Contribute to ML infrastructure decisions and best practices across the engineering org

 - Stay current on LLM advances, retrieval techniques, and ML tooling; bring insights back to the team



Profile

We’re looking for someone who is interested in building the AI user experience for the future. We punch above our weight so we value extreme ownership, accountability, and proactiveness. The ideal candidate is a master craftsman who enjoys building great products with LLMs at scale.

You are a good fit if you have:

 - 4+ years in ML/AI engineering, with significant experience shipping production systems

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals: you write clean, maintainable code

 - Deep experience with retrieval, ranking, RAG, or search systems at scale

 - Hands on proficiency with PyTorch, vector databases, and modern ML infrastructure

 - Experience with LLM applications (prompt engineering, fine tuning, eval frameworks)

 - Product thinker: understands how to connect great ML products to constraints, evals, and modeling when building them

 - A bias toward action: you ship fast, measure rigorously, and iterate based on data

 - Ownership mentality (no task is too small for you) – it’s all hands on deck here and we need everyone rowing the boat.

 - Excellent communication skills and a collaborative mindset – we’re extremely lean so we’re looking for a team player who works well with others.

 - Type-A tendencies: maybe you’re extremely detail-oriented. Or maybe you take pride in shipping high-quality products. Whatever it is you do, you give it your all.

 - Must be based in or willing to relocate to SF – although we’re flexible with days off & schedules, we have a 100% in-office culture during the week.

And even if you don’t fall neatly into any of these buckets, we’re looking for scrappy and motivated self-starters above all. Hustle is expected, grit is required.



Perks

 - Competitive salary and generous equity package

 - Health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Flexible PTO and parental leave

 - Paid team lunches during the week

 - Relocation package

source_jobs.title: Member of Design Staff
source_jobs.description:
Highlight is building a shared intelligence layer for the modern workforce. Highlight unifies context across every person and tool on your team, seamlessly bridging information silos. It evolves with your organization to proactively route knowledge and reliably automate workflows.



The Role

We’re hiring a Member of Design Staff with technical skills to own visual craft and product design at Highlight.

You’ll help define how people interact with AI beyond chatboxes, and your work will be visible immediately in the product. If successful, your designs will be the reason people screenshot Highlight, share it, and talk about how innovative it feels.

You’ll take raw interaction ideas, half-formed concepts, and functional wireframes and turn them into interfaces that feel inevitable, futuristic, and obsessively polished. This role is about execution at the highest level: pixels, motion, transitions, hierarchy, and interaction details that make complex AI systems feel simple, powerful, and delightful. You’ll work directly with product, and engineering to bring new interaction models to life fast – often going from idea to high-fidelity prototype in hours, not weeks.

This is a hands-on role. You’ll design and prototype constantly, push the team toward higher taste and sharper decisions, and set the bar for what “great” looks like in AI-native software.

Note: this is an on-site role, requiring five days a week in our office.



Job Responsibilities

 - Own visual and interaction quality across Highlight’s product

 - Regularly talk to users, find creative solutions to their problems, and incorporate them into your designs

 - Partner closely with the Head of Design to translate interaction concepts, product direction, and rough wireframes into confident, opinionated, high-fidelity UI

 - Collaborate daily with engineers to bring polished interfaces, motion, and micro-interactions into production

 - Rapidly prototype AI-native interaction models, through click dummys and in code, often moving from idea to testable prototype within hours

 - Design and refine micro-interactions, animations, transitions, and layout systems that make complex AI behavior feel intuitive and delightful

 - Push beyond existing UI conventions and help define new patterns for how users interact with AI systems

 - Set and uphold a high bar for visual craft, consistency, and accessibility across the product



Profile

We’re looking for someone who wants to define what the AI user experience looks like next. We punch above our weight and expect extreme ownership, accountability, and proactiveness by default.

The ideal candidate is world-class at visual and interaction design, with deep experience designing desktop interfaces and a proven track record of shipping outstanding UI at scale. You obsess over pixels, motion, spacing, and clarity. You’re comfortable with ambiguity and turn half-formed ideas into confident, opinionated interfaces. You move fast without lowering the bar, and your work consistently shows exceptional craft.



You are a good fit if:

 - You have shipped products users obsess over

 - You are exceptional at turning rough interaction concepts and wireframes into polished, production-ready interfaces

 - You can’t get enough of micro-interactions, animation, and interaction details that elevate the entire product

 - You are deeply plugged into state-of-the-art UI and actively experiment beyond existing design conventions

 - You’ve independently designed and shipped work at the level of the Dia (Mac) onboarding, Lapse (iOS), or corner maps (iOS). That level of craft and product judgment is the baseline for this role. Familiarity with work like this is expected.

 - You can prototype advanced interactions quickly, and are either technical yourself or fluent with tools like Cursor or Claude Code to realize ideas without waiting on others

 - You have experience with designing AI-native interaction patterns

 - You send us your work and

notes: Highly technical job postings with emphasis on AI and ML engineering.
